mission.title: argo-rollouts: Ability to control stable scale when using traffic routing
mission.description: Ability to control stable scale when using traffic routing. Requested by 35+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-rollouts deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-rollouts version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
helm list -n argo-rollouts 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-rollouts installation.

steps.title: Review argo-rollouts configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-rollouts configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get configmap -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-rollouts
```
When using traffic routing with canary, the stable stack is never scaled down, until the rollout is fully promoted. This means that we are double the number of total replicas in the environment eventually (when the traffic weight reaches 100).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ability to control stable scale when using traffic routing
steps.description:
Checklist:
```yaml
spec:
  strategy:
    canary:
      dynamicStableScale: true
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get events -n argo-rollouts --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Ability to control stable scale when using traffic routing" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/argoproj/argo-rollouts/pull/1382
codeSnippets:
spec:
  strategy:
    canary:
      stableScale: Full | Dynamic  # Names are TBD (Full == existing behavior of leaving stable scaled to 100%, Dynamic == inverse of canary weight)

So the updated sequence of events during abort using dynamic scaling is

0. Before the abort, at the pause step, we started at:
`stable: 1/1, canary: 3/3, desiredWeight: 75, actualWeight: 75`

1. Upon abort, we immediately scale up stable to 100%. The desired weight will become 0, but actual weight remains untouched initially:
`stable: 1/4, canary: 3/3, desiredWeight: 0, actualWeight: 75`

2. As the stable pods become available, we adjust traffic weight according to stable pod availability. Let's say 2/4 stable pods are available. The actual weight would be set to 50.
`stable: 2/4, canary: 3/3, desiredWeight: 0, actualWeight: 50`

3. Now that actual weight decreased to 50, the canary scale is reduced to 2
`stable: 2/4, canary: 2/2, desiredWeight: 0, actualWeight: 50`

4. when 3/4 stable po

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-rollouts installed or the issue environment reproducible.
